Introduction: worship undefined in Bible; need to understand ritual context
Neolithic sites: Jericho, Göbekli Tepe, Çatalhöyük as earliest settlements
Ritual sites preceded settlements; pilgrimage gave rise to agriculture
Spiritual experience at sites; shift in scholarly consensus
Biblical parallels: Jacob at Bethel, Joshua's stones, church building on holy sites
Çatalhöyük: burials under floors, grave goods, animal remains indicate sacrifice
Bull imagery; Behemoth as spirit of tyranny; etymology and meaning
Goddess figures; Leviathan as spirit of chaos; connection to sea monsters
Behemoth and Leviathan as primary spirits opposed to God; evidence at Çatalhöyük
Question: why worship such spirits?
Answer: hospitality as survival strategy; cannot conquer demons, so make peace
Transition to ancient ritual texts; how they were written (stories, not rubrics)
Caller Ryan: question about angelic apostasy and its material manifestations
Response: near-universal fall of angels assigned to nations; exception of Michael; material side (agriculture, cities)
Greco-Roman sacrifices: continuity with Near Eastern practices
Anatomy of sacrifice: procession, meal as hospitality, food offerings
Procession details: incense, music, animals; Psalms of Ascent
Sanctification: laying on of hands, designation of elements
Animal's willingness: tuft of hair cut and burned; killing not ritualized
Blood collection; hero graves; Odyssey example of blood attracting shades
Butchering and division of portions; altar as table (Latin 'ara')
Burning of offerings; touching and elevating; feast follows
Caller Cristiana: guardian angels; response on their nature and role
Guardian angel carries soul to heaven; 40-day journey
When do we get guardian angels? Baptism; angelic relationship transcends time
One guardian angel; also patron saints; overlap
Feast after burning; trapeza; communal meal creates koinonia
Background to Paul's warning about idol feasts and communion with demons
Role of priests as ritual technicians; divination; example of heartless animal
Incense: pleasing aroma and purification; fumigation rituals; demonic cockroaches
Etymology: Greek 'kathairo' from Semitic 'qtr'; incense and cleansing
Propitiation vs. expiation: two aspects of sacrifice
Conclusion: ritual reconnects with spiritual reality; Orthodox worship as participation
Ritual as fourth mode of interaction with reality; way to connect with God
Pre-show, station ID, host introductions, call-in info
Banter about weather, cold, and pronunciation difficulties
Extended banter about Stonehenge, Facebook pictures, and personal stories
Restatement of nomadic vs. settled life; redundant explanation
Modern parallel (Trinity-St. Sergius) and commentary on mobility; tangential
Further commentary on modern displacement; not advancing argument
Transition filler
Single line; redundant

Jokes about cancel culture and social media; off-topic banter
Break, sponsor ad, call-in reminder

Tolkien reference and banter; not essential to argument
